rows.v: Rice, Wheat, Corn (Maize)
Everyday sides, breads, and the base of meals worldwide — over half of all calories consumed.
Rice, wheat, and corn together supply more than half of all the calories consumed by humans worldwide — making them, by a wide margin, the most important plants in the human diet.
Quinoa, Farro, Freekeh, Amaranth
Grain bowls, salads, and gluten-free baking — heirloom nutrition with a satisfying chew.
Quinoa isn't technically a grain at all — it's an edible seed that's prepared and eaten just like one, and it's one of the few plant foods that contains all nine essential amino acids.
Oats, Barley, Bulgur, Sorghum
Breakfast cereals, soups, and stews — high fiber that supports digestion and steady energy.
The Dietary Guidelines for Americans recommend that at least half of all grains eaten daily be whole grains — roughly 2 to 4 servings a day.
Millet, Teff
Porridge, flatbreads, and rice substitutes — tiny grains, big nutrition.
Because teff grains are so tiny, a much larger share of each grain is nutrient-rich bran and germ compared to larger grains — making it one of the most nutrient-dense grains by weight.
Kale, Collard Greens, Bok Choy, Cabbage
Roasted, braised, or fermented — sturdy leaves that stand up to bold cooking.
One cup of raw collard greens contains over 1000% of the daily value for vitamin K — more than almost any other common leafy green.
Romaine Lettuce, Arugula, Watercress, Endive, Microgreens
Raw, crisp, and fresh — the base of salads and a finishing touch on countless dishes.
Despite looking like a delicate garnish, microgreens can pack a higher concentration of vitamins and phytochemicals per gram than the full-grown vegetable they come from.

groups.name: Cooking & Root-Top Greens

rows.v: Spinach, Swiss Chard, Beet Greens, Turnip Greens
Sautéed, wilted, or simmered — and don't toss the stems, they're edible too.
Beet greens, Swiss chard stems, and turnip greens are often thrown away — but all three are edible, nutrient-dense parts of vegetables most people only eat half of.

groups.name_es: Verduras De Cocción Y Hojas De Raíz
